payload.text: Build me an website with screens that look like this. You can hotlink images from the html.Build me an website with screens that look like this. You can hotlink images from the html,make it so it has a drag and drop system that allows me to add books,add a log in system where the admin log in user is admin and the password for the admin is 123admin,also add a admin pannel on the left where the admin can add the books and everthing,hence the drag and drop files upload for the covers,make it send an email ater u have borrowed a book and say that if u dont return it withing 7 day period,u will get a fine,add the stock number and also make the admin be able to add the genre and author of the book,make the homepage show the book cover,the tittle and the autor below it,make it elegant and minimalistic,with an elegent font

**Defining the Athenaeum**

I'm now zeroing in on the core aesthetic vision for "Athenaeum." I'm visualizing a minimalist React application. The aim is to capture a sense of refined simplicity, with an elegant user interface that's visually captivating. I'm focusing on incorporating the specific color palette requested, using the fuschia primary and the vintage beige for a compelling design.


**Refining the UI Components**

I'm now outlining key UI components. I'm planning a book listing with cover, title, and author. Details will pop up in a modal-like side panel. There will be a sidebar for Genres and Authors. I've also planned an admin login system with credentials `admin` and `123admin`, as well as an admin panel integrated into the sidebar that includes a form for adding books, with image upload. I'm focusing on "Borrow" actions and simulated email feedback.


**Outlining the File Structure**

I'm now concretizing the project structure. I'm focusing on key files such as `index.tsx`, `App.tsx` for routing and state management, and `types.ts` for interfaces. The plan is to create components: `Sidebar`, `BookCard`, `BookDetails`, `AdminPanel`, `LoginModal`, and `FileUpload`. I'm building a `bookService.ts` for borrowing and adding functionalities.

payload.text:
Design and implement an admin dashboard. This panel should include features for adding new books, specifying their title, author, genre, and stock quantity. It should also include a drag-and-drop interface for uploading book cover images.

Design the website's homepage to display book covers prominently, with the book title and author listed clearly below each cover. Use an elegant and minimalistic design with a sophisticated font.

Implement a book borrowing system. When a user borrows a book, send an email confirmation stating that the book must be returned within 7 days to avoid a fine. Ensure the stock count is updated accordingly.

Design and implement an admin dashboard. This panel should include features for adding new books, specifying their title, author, genre, and stock quantity. It should also include a drag-and-drop interface for uploading book cover images.

Design and implement an admin dashboard. This panel should include features for adding new books, specifying their title, author, genre, and stock quantity. It should also include a drag-and-drop interface for uploading book cover images.


add a borrow and return system for after u have borrowed a  book, also let the admin see who has logged in,also a a log in page,change the name to Biblioteka,make it in albanian,add a book logo that fits the aesthetic,minimal and elegent

**Updating the Application Name**

Okay, I've started the process by updating all text instances to reflect the new name, "Biblioteka," including the HTML title and sidebar. Next, I'll translate all of the UI text into Albanian, focusing on labels and buttons first. I think this will really improve the user experience.


**Adding Return Functionality**

I'm now integrating a "Return" feature, requiring me to track borrowed books locally using `borrowedBookIds`. Next on my list is simulating a "Login History" in the admin panel. I'm also thinking about the best way to handle the login page; dedicated view or prominent modal? After that, I'll incorporate a suitable book logo, and translate the Admin panel items. I also need to update the notification message.


**Implementing Return Functionality**

I'm making headway! I've incorporated the "Kthe" (Return) logic, and added the "Kthe Librin" (Return Book) button in `BookDetails.tsx`. I've updated the `borrowedBookIds` state in `App.tsx` and started translating strings across the UI. I also renamed the sidebar labels and the app title to their new Albanian equivalents. I updated `index.html` as well. Currently, I'm working on the `LoginHistory` addition for the admin panel, as well as translating all the text in the admin panel and in Book details. I'm working on the 7-day penalty message now.

payload.text: add the log in page as the first thing u see,add an inbox pannel for the email,plus make it an albanian authentic language ,keep it simple and clean

**Prioritizing Initial Login**

Okay, I'm focusing on the "Biblioteka" app modifications. The primary goal is to shift the initial view to the login page. I'm also considering the implications this has on user navigation. I'm moving beyond that, to the inbox panel for emails, focusing on its potential use, possibly for admins to view a history.


**Refining Notification System**

I'm now zeroing in on the email notifications. I've updated `types.ts` to include an `Email` type. In `App.tsx`, I'm adding state for these emails and implementing logic to "send" them upon borrowing, so I can test the "inbox" function. The plan includes the inbox for admins, likely as a dedicated component or view state. I'll translate the menu to authentic Albanian.

payload.text: add a settings pannel where users can change between 5 themes all elegant and different(blue and orange jeapanese style)(red and black and white japanese style)(purple and pink japanese styel)(black and white japanese style ) etc

**Designing the UI**

I'm currently focused on the UI design for a new settings panel within "Biblioteka." The goal is to provide seamless theme selection. My work concentrates on the UI for users to switch between 3 elegant Japanese-style themes. Specifically, Blue/Orange, Red/Black/White, and Purple/Pink are the initial design targets.


**Expanding Theme Palette**

I've been expanding the theme palette for the settings panel in "Biblioteka," adding a Black and White Japanese-style theme. I'm also considering a Matcha theme as well. I'm focusing on updating `types.ts` and `App.tsx` to include theme states. I've also begun creating `SettingsPanel.tsx`, and plan to update style applications and the sidebar next.


**Defining New Themes**

I'm currently fleshing out a new theme featuring green and gold accents against a warm sand background. I've updated `types.ts` to include the `Theme` interface and `SETTINGS` view, and am working on `App.tsx` to handle the theme state with CSS variables and the new view. The "Cilësimet" (Settings) button is in place in `Sidebar.tsx`. I've also created `SettingsPanel.tsx`, and I'm planning to move on to refining the Albanian text as well, including "Tema" for theme. I'm focusing on cleaning up `index.html` as well. I'm focusing on defining the themes in a constant. Ao & Daidai (Blue/Orange) primary: #0f4c81 (Classic Blue)


**Refining Theme Definitions**

I'm now zeroing in on defining the theme color values. I've compiled the hex codes for the primary, secondary, and background colors for Kurenai, Sakura, Sumi-e, and Matcha themes, and am working on incorporating these color definitions into `index.html`. I'll be using CSS variables for `primary`, `bg`, etc., to allow dynamic theme application.

payload.text: make it so that when something is done,it automaticlly stores it automaticlly in the computer running it

**Considering Persistent Storage**

I'm focusing on persistent data storage within the current environment. My initial thought is to utilize `localStorage` for automatic, local saving of data. This looks like the most appropriate solution to ensure data is retained in the browser.


**Verifying Data Persistence**

I've been re-examining data persistence to ensure complete coverage. The existing `App.tsx` implementation already leverages `localStorage` for automatic saving of critical data. I've broken down the key actions – adding books, borrowing, logins, and settings changes – to confirm that `useEffect` hooks reliably persist the relevant state updates (`books`, `borrowedBookIds`, `loginHistory`, `sentEmails`, and `theme`) to `localStorage`. I'm verifying all "done" items are indeed covered.


**Confirming Implicit Persistence**

I'm now completely satisfied that all critical state updates automatically trigger `localStorage` saves. Reviewing `handleAddBook`, `handleBorrow`, and `setTheme` confirms that state changes to `books`, `borrowedBookIds`, `loginHistory`, `sentEmails`, and `theme` are reliably persisted. The `useEffect` hooks work as designed, so no manual "Save" trigger seems necessary or expected. I'm focusing on clarifying how initial state is loaded.


**Refining Storage Consistency**

I'm now zeroing in on data persistence consistency. I've pinpointed a key area for improvement: inconsistent `localStorage` keys across different data types. I'm going to standardize them using a consistent prefix. To address any potential concerns about immediate saves, I'll add a check to verify that data is indeed being saved to `localStorage`, in addition to my previous work. I am not seeing any requirements to add a visual feedback for this, so I will omit this.


**Enhancing Data Persistence**

I'm now refining the user experience around data persistence. While automatic saving works, I plan to add a subtle "Data is stored locally" label, in Albanian, in the UI. Also, I will be updating and refining the `INITIAL_BOOKS` loading to prevent overwrites, only loading if no data exists. Finally, I will standardize `localStorage` keys to include a prefix such as `biblioteka_`.

payload.text: make it so that i can remove books, only by the admin

**Considering Admin Controls**

I'm currently focused on implementing a feature to remove books from the collection, but this action needs to be limited to the admin user. My plan involves updating `App.tsx` with a `handleRemoveBook` function, and then either modifying the existing `AdminPanel` or creating a dedicated section for managing and removing existing books. This will ensure that only authorized users can perform this operation.


**Refining Removal Method**

I've considered several deletion options, and I'm leaning toward implementing a delete button within the `BookDetails` component that will only display when an admin is logged in. This approach provides a balance of accessibility and control, allowing admins to remove books directly while browsing. I'll need to pass the `isAdmin` prop to `BookDetails` to handle the visibility of this button and the `onRemoveBook` prop for the deletion function itself.

entries.description: Implement handleRemoveBook and pass necessary props to AdminPanel and BookDetails to allow book deletion by admins.
